text: Similarly, subsequent principal component directions obtained maximizing variance projection YY onto direction, orthogonal previously chosen directions. k≥2k \geq 2, vk=argmax∥v∥2=1v⊤Σvsubject tov⊤vj=0,j=1,…,k−1. v_k = \arg\max_{\|v\|_2 = 1} v^\top \Sigma v \quad \text{subject } \quad v^\top v_j = 0, \qquad j = 1,\ldots,k-1. title: Principal Component Analysis Background
text: see PCA directions eigenvectors, consider first principal component problem max∥v∥2=1v⊤Σv. \max_{\|v\|_2 = 1} v^\top \Sigma v. Using constraint v⊤v=1v^\top v = 1, define Lagrangian ℒ(v,λ)=v⊤Σv−λ(v⊤v−1). \mathcal{L}(v,\lambda) = v^\top \Sigma v - \lambda (v^\top v - 1). Taking derivative respect vv setting equal zero gives ∂ℒ∂v=2Σv−2λv=0. \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial v} = 2\Sigma v - 2\lambda v = 0. Therefore, Σv=λv. \Sigma v = \lambda v. Hence, optimizer vv must eigenvector Σ\Sigma, corresponding Lagrange multiplier λ\lambda associated eigenvalue. unit eigenvector vv, v⊤Σv=v⊤(λv)=λv⊤v=λ. v^\top \Sigma v = v^\top (\lambda v) = \lambda v^\top v = \lambda. Thus, max∥v∥2=1Var(v⊤Y)=max∥v∥2=1v⊤Σv=λmax. \max_{\|v\|_2 = 1} \operatorname{Var}(v^\top Y) = \max_{\|v\|_2 = 1} v^\top \Sigma v = \lambda_{\max}. first principal component direction eigenvector corresponding largest eigenvalue λ1\lambda_1, maximum projected variance λ1\lambda_1. Repeating procedure orthogonality constraints gives remaining eigenvectors. Therefore, Σvj=λjvj,j=1,…,p, \Sigma v_j = \lambda_j v_j, \qquad j = 1,\ldots,p,  λ1≥λ2≥⋯≥λp≥0. \lambda_1 \geq \lambda_2 \geq \cdots \geq \lambda_p \geq 0. ℓ\ell-th eigenvalue can interpreted variance projected random variable along ℓ\ell-th principal component direction λℓ=vℓ⊤Σvℓ=Var(vℓ⊤Y). \lambda_\ell = v_\ell^\top \Sigma v_\ell = \operatorname{Var}(v_\ell^\top Y).

title: dppca
text: dppca provides tools differentially private principal component analysis (PCA) visualization. package focuses privacy-preserving versions common PCA exploratory tools, including PC direction estimation, scree/PVE plots, score plots, histogram-based summaries PCA scores. main goal dppca make easy compare non-private PCA summaries differentially private alternatives keeping interface close standard PCA workflow R.

what: 2. Differentially private scree and PVE plots
title: dppca
text: dp_scree() computes private scree values proportions variance explained using one several private estimators. Available methods : "clipped": clipped mean based estimator "pmwm": private modified winsorized mean based estimator "huber": Huber-type robust estimator Method-specific tuning parameters supplied control helper functions: draw private scree/PVE plot, use dp_scree_plot(): Use type = "pve" plot proportions variance explained, type = "scree" plot raw scree values. Plot styling handled internally user-facing interface stays simple.
code: res <- dp_scree(   X,   k = 5,   method = "pmwm",   eps_total = 1,   delta_total = 1e-6,   center = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   control = pmwm_control(a = 0, b = 10) ) clipped_control(C_clip = 3)  pmwm_control(   beta = 1.01,   a = 0,   b = 10,   trim_const = 10,   eta = 0.01,   split_mode = TRUE )  huber_control(   mu0 = 0,   eta0 = 1,   T = 50,   M = 20,   k_min_m2 = -40,   k_max_m2 = 40,   m2_frac = 1 / 4 ) dp_scree_plot(   X,   k = 5,   dp_scree_method = "all",   eps_total = 1,   delta_total = 1e-6,   center = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   type = "pve",   control = list(     clipped = clipped_control(C_clip = 3),     pmwm = pmwm_control(a = 0, b = 10),     huber = huber_control(T = 50, M = 20)   ) )

text: typical analysis consists following steps.
code: library(dppca)  # 1. Load or prepare a numeric data matrix X <- as.matrix(gau)  # 2. Estimate private PC directions V_dp <- dp_pc_dir(   X,   k = 2,   eps_total = 1,   delta_total = 1e-6,   center = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE )  # 3. Plot private PVE curves pve_res <- dp_scree_plot(   X,   k = 5,   dp_scree_method = "all",   eps_total = 1,   delta_total = 1e-6,   type = "pve",   control = list(     clipped = clipped_control(C_clip = 3),     pmwm = pmwm_control(a = 0, b = 10),     huber = huber_control(T = 50, M = 20)   ) )  # 4. Draw a private score plot score_plot <- dp_score_plot(   X,   k = 2,   eps_total = 1,   delta_total = 1e-6,   bin_method = "WZ" )  score_plot  dp_score_plot_group(   X = gau_g,   G = "color",   eps_total = 5,   delta_total = 1e-6,   center = TRUE,   standardize = FALSE,   bin_method = "WZ" )

title: Estimate principal component directions — dp_pc_dir

title: Differentially private score histograms — dp_score
text: Let \(v_a\) \(v_b\) principal component directions selected axes = c(, b) \(1 \le < b \le ncol(X)\). preprocessing, score point \(\)th observation \(s_i = (x_i^\top v_a, x_i^\top v_b)\). non-private score plot display points \(s_1, \ldots, s_n\) directly. function instead summarizes empirical distribution two-dimensional histogram releases private versions histogram visualization. fixed_frame = NULL, plotting frame constructed privately. two score coordinates stacked one vector, private lower upper quantiles estimated using smooth-sensitivity based quantile mechanism (Nissim et al. 2007) , resulting interval used common square frame axes. fixed_frame supplied, treated public privacy budget spent frame construction. private histogram computed rectangular grid defined fixed_frame private frame bin counts bins. row-level adjacency, changing one observation can increase one bin count one decrease another one, giving \(\ell_1\) sensitivity \(2\) \(\ell_2\) sensitivity \(\sqrt{2}\) count vector. Two private histogram mechanisms supported: "add" constructs additive differentially private histogram adding Gaussian noise bin counts, clipping negative noisy counts zero, normalizing result. additive-noise approach commonly used private histograms; see Wasserman Zhou (2010) . "sparse" constructs sparse differentially private histogram settings many bins empty. perturbs nonzero empirical bin proportions keeps bins whose noisy values exceed stability threshold, following stability-based private histogram idea Karwa Vadhan (2017) . privacy parameters allocated across privacy-consuming steps. g_dppca = FALSE fixed_frame = NULL, half eps delta used private frame construction half private histogram. g_dppca = TRUE fixed_frame = NULL, parameters split equally among private direction estimation, private frame construction, private histogram release. fixed_frame supplied, frame step skipped remaining steps split privacy parameters equally. detailed procedure mathematical formulations, refer https://yejinjo0220.github.io/dppca/articles/dp_score.

title: Differentially private scree values — dp_scree
text: Let \(X\) denote preprocessed data matrix let \(v_l\) \(l\)th principal component direction. \(l\)th score vector \(z_l = X v_l\). corresponding sample scree value can written $$   \hat{\lambda}_l   = v_l^\top \widehat{\Sigma} v_l   = \frac{1}{n - 1}\sum_{= 1}^n z_{il}^2   = \frac{n}{n - 1}\left(\frac{1}{n}\sum_{= 1}^n w_{il}\right),   \qquad w_{il} = z_{il}^2. $$ Therefore, scree value estimated privately estimating mean \(w_{1l}, \ldots, w_{nl}\) multiplying \(n/(n - 1)\). supported methods differ private mean estimated: "clipped" clips squared scores \(w_{\ell}\) C_clip applies Gaussian mechanism (Dwork Roth 2014) . simplest option depends directly clipping threshold. "pmwm" uses private modified winsorized mean approach Ramsay Spicker (2025) , adapted accompanying Python implementation R. privately estimates tail cutoffs, winsorizes squared scores \(w_{\ell}\), releases noisy winsorized mean. "huber" uses Huber-type private robust mean estimator based noisy gradient descent, following Yu et al. (2024) . argument g_dppca controls principal component directions obtained. g_dppca = FALSE, directions computed non-privately eigenvector sample covariance, full privacy parameters eps delta used private scree value estimation. g_dppca = TRUE, directions computed privately using dp_pc_dir(). case, privacy parameters split equally: eps / 2 delta / 2 used private direction estimation, remaining eps / 2 delta / 2 used private scree value estimation. mono = TRUE, final monotone adjustment post-processing step change privacy guarantee. detailed procedure mathematical formulations, refer https://yejinjo0220.github.io/dppca/articles/dp_scree.

code: data(gau, package = "dppca")  # Use a small subset to keep the example fast. data(gau, package = "dppca") X <- gau[1:200, ]  # Draw a private scree plot using the clipped mean method. set.seed(123) dp_scree_plot(   X,   k = 5,   method = "clipped",   control = clipped_control(C_clip = 3),   eps = 3,   delta = 1e-3 )   # Multiple scree methods can be overlaid by passing a vector to `method` # and a named list to `control`, for example: # dp_scree_plot( #   X, #   k = 5, #   method = c("clipped", "pmwm", "huber"), #   control = list( #     clipped = clipped_control(C_clip = 3), #     pmwm = pmwm_control(a = 0, b = 50, trim_const = 10, eta = 0.01), #     huber = huber_control(k_min_m2 = -10, k_max_m2 = 10, m2_frac = 1 / 4) #   ), #   eps = 3, #   delta = 1e-3 # )
